extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r2, which fires when elderly AND g6pd_deficiency AND heart_failure. rec_drug_b is set true only by r3, which fires when elderly. These conditions overlap: any input with elderly=true, g6pd_deficiency=true, heart_failure=true satisfies both. Since r2 is the only rule touching rec_drug_a and r3 is the only rule touching rec_drug_b, priorities and ordering are irrelevant (they govern competing rules on the same output, but here the two rules target different outputs). With elderly=true, g6pd_deficiency=true, heart_failure=true: r2 sets rec_drug_a=true and r3 sets rec_drug_b=true, making (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) true. This violates the mutual-exclusion property. immunosuppressed only affects rec_drug_c (r1) and is irrelevant; set to false.
